_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, composed of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices indexed to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 132, indicating a roughly 32% increase since 2015. This rise reflects an uneven and relatively recent climb rather than steady inflation across the board. The index aggregates the prices of these common breakfast commodities to provide a snapshot of their overall cost trends. It serves as a concise measure of price dynamics in this segment of soft commodities.
notes.movers: Price increases are mainly driven by coffee and orange juice, with coffee up by 93% and orange juice up by 22% since 2015, while wheat and sugar experienced smaller gains of 7% and 5% respectively. These changes are largely due to weather and crop disease factors—such as frosts and droughts affecting arabica coffee regions, and citrus greening plus hurricanes impacting orange juice supplies. Because soft commodities are traded in thin markets, where supply shocks can sharply move prices, these specific crop events have outsized effects compared to broad-based inflation.
notes.why: The overall rise in the breakfast basket index is predominantly influenced by a few weather-sensitive crops subject to disease and adverse climatic conditions. Such factors can cause persistent price elevations over multiple years due to supply constraints. This underscores the vulnerability of soft commodity markets to environmental risks rather than generalized economic inflation.
